overview: A thin orchestration layer designed to provide coding agents with long-term memory, architectural context, and modular ownership. It utilizes Cursor's native primitives (.mdc files) and a write-back loop to ensure consistency across sessions and multi-agent coordination.

requirements.title: Ownership Registry & Agent Management
requirements.description: Establish a centralized system to define agent roles and map specific code modules to dedicated agent teams.
success_criteria: CLI command `ace own <path> --agent <id>` correctly maps a directory to an agent in `.ace/ownership.yaml`.
CLI command `ace who <file>` correctly identifies the owner using longest-prefix matching.
Agent metadata is persistently stored in `.ace/agents.yaml` including ID, name, role, and responsibilities.

sub_requirements.title: Agent Creation
sub_requirements.description: Mechanism to create named agents manually or autonomously.
success_criteria: New agents are appended to `agents.yaml` with unique IDs and dedicated `.mdc` memory files.

requirements.title: Context Builder & Memory Slicing
requirements.description: Dynamically compose the context injected into the agent's prompt based on the specific task, owner, and architectural history.
success_criteria: Context composition includes `_global.mdc`, the specific `<role>.mdc`, and recent ADRs.
Task-specific framing (implement, review, debug) is prepended to the final prompt.
Token usage is optimized by only including relevant subsystem context (Contextual Specialization).

requirements.title: Write-back Pipeline
requirements.description: An automated reflection process that extracts lessons learned and architectural decisions from agent output and updates the playbook.
success_criteria: Orchestrator runs a reflection prompt against agent output post-task.
Successful tasks increment `helpful` counters in `.mdc` files; failures increment `harmful` counters.
New strategies or pitfalls are appended to the `.mdc` file in the correct section without overwriting existing content.

requirements.title: RALPH Loop Engine (Iterative Loop)
requirements.description: A Reasoning-Action-Learning-Progress-Halt motor that drives the agent until tests pass or max iterations are reached.
success_criteria: The `ace loop` command executes the agent and evaluates results against a provided test command.
The system performs a 'Context Refresh' between iterations to incorporate failures as new learnings.
The loop halts automatically when tests pass or the `--max` iteration count is hit.

requirements.title: Agent Messaging System (Agent Mail)
requirements.description: Internal asynchronous communication system allowing agents to exchange context, proposals, and coordination messages.
success_criteria: Each agent has a functional `inbox/` and `sent/` folder in `.ace/mail/` using Markdown files.
Messages support threading via `thread_id` to maintain conversation context.
Agents can 'attach' files or code snippets to messages for shared review.

requirements.title: Multi-Agent Consensus & Debate
requirements.description: A protocol for multiple agents to resolve conflicts when changes cross ownership boundaries.
success_criteria: Conflict detection flags tasks requiring multiple owners.
Consensus-flow triggers an LLM-referee to evaluate debates and reach a decision.
Escalation to human users occurs if consensus isn't reached within 3 rounds.

requirements.title: Token Consumption Modes
requirements.description: Global settings to control the depth of agent reasoning and debate to manage API costs.
success_criteria: Switching to 'Low' mode disables multi-agent debate and minimizes context.
Switching to 'High' mode enables pro-active refactoring suggestions and deep context analysis.

requirements.title: Google Stitch Integration
requirements.description: Integration with Google Labs AI-native design tools for UI/UX mockup generation and code extraction.
success_criteria: Agent can generate 'Vibe Design' prompts to be used with Stitch.
CLI command `ace ui sync <url>` extracts Tailwind/Flutter code from a Stitch canvas into the local codebase.

requirements.title: Global Project Memory (AGENTS.md)
requirements.description: Maintain a root-level markdown file to provide cross-tool context for all agents and human developers.
success_criteria: File contains a table of agent roles, responsibilities, and rule file paths.
Architectural decisions (ADRs) are summarized and updated automatically.
The file is readable and usable by non-ACE tools (Cursor, Claude Code, Copilot).

requirements.title: Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)
requirements.description: Formalize agent interactions through predefined workflows for common tasks.
success_criteria: Implementation of an 'Onboarding' SOP that generates a status summary for new agents.
Implementation of a 'QA-audit' SOP where agents scan subsystems for DRY/YAGNI violations.
